news.title: Brigade Hotel Ventures post net profit of ₹64.59 crore in FY26
news.para: Brigade Hotel Ventures has announced a remarkable 173 percent surge in its net consolidated profit for the financial year 2025-26. The company's profit after tax reached ₹64.59 crore. Total income also saw a healthy increase. This strong performance was driven by improved average room rates and revenue per available room across its hotel portfolio.

news.title: Ghaziabad misses stamp duty target by 14% in FY26
news.para: The stamp and registration department in Ghaziabad closed the financial year 2025–26 with a revenue collection of Rs 2,963 crore, achieving 86% of its annual target of Rs 3480 crore

news.title: Greenply Industries' net profit dips 2.13% in FY26; CEO resigns
news.para: Greenply Industries saw its net profit decline slightly in FY26. However, total income grew by over 10 percent. The fourth quarter showed robust performance with income up nearly 19 percent. The plywood business delivered strong volume growth. The MDF segment also achieved significant sales growth, marking its first fully operational quarter post-expansion.

news.title: Adoption of Nagpur's eco-friendly property scheme remains below 1%
news.para: Nagpur's green property scheme adoption remains below one percent. Despite incentives, awareness and costs limit participation. Data shows a recent increase in beneficiaries, with many opting for multiple eco-friendly measures. This initiative aims to address climate stress, but wider adoption is crucial for the city's sustainability goals.

news.title: Mahindra Lifespace Developers' posts ₹298.17 crore profit in FY26
news.para: Mahindra Lifespace Developers announced a substantial profit of ₹298.17 crore for FY26, a significant jump from FY25. Total income also saw a sharp rise to ₹1,265.96 crore. The company successfully raised funds through a rights issue for debt repayment and land acquisition.

news.title: Meghna Infracon to invest up to ₹500 crore in five Mumbai redevelopment projects
news.para: Meghna Infracon Infrastructure plans to invest ₹400-500 crore to develop five redevelopment projects across Mumbai and Thane, with an estimated combined GDV of ₹600 crore, as it expands its presence in premium micro-markets.

news.title: Dalmia Bharat's net profit up 65.52% in FY26
news.para: Dalmia Bharat announced a 65.52 percent jump in its net consolidated profit for the financial year 2025-26. The company's profit after tax reached ₹1,157 crore. Total income also saw a rise. Cement volumes improved, and the company recommended a final dividend of ₹5 per equity share.

news.title: FIR against six officials in Beed’s Kej nagar panchayat over ₹4.2 crore PMAY scam
news.para: A Rs 4.2 crore fraud under the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (PMAY) has been uncovered in Beed district, leading to an FIR against six officials. An audit revealed funds meant for beneficiaries were illegally diverted to unrelated accounts between 2021 and 2022. Investigations suggest missing financial records and transfers to personal accounts.

news.title: KSH Infra plans ₹1,250 crore AIF for industrial, logistics assets
news.para: KSH Infra plans to launch a Category II AIF with a target corpus of ₹1,250 crore, with an additional ₹500 crore through co-investments, to invest in industrial and logistics assets across key cities.

news.title: No more extension for RMC plants that fail to comply with environmental norms in Pune: MPCB chief
news.para: The Maharashtra Pollution Control Board will no longer extend deadlines for ready-mix concrete plants failing to meet environmental standards. Around 300 plants in Pune have halted operations due to coordination issues. The MPCB chairman met with plant owners, assuring support for compliance. Efforts are underway to create a unified procedure for plant installation and operation.

news.title: Domestic investors accounts for 76% of real estate inflows in Q1 2026: Report
news.para: Domestic institutional investors accounted for 76% of total real estate inflows in Q1 2026, continuing their dominance over foreign capital for the third consecutive quarter. Total investments stood at $1.6 billion, up 26% year-on-year.

news.title: Joint panels to speed up pending Nashik smart city projects
news.para: The Nashik Municipal Corporation (NMC) is forming joint committees with the Smart City Corporation to expedite the completion of over Rs700 crore worth of pending projects. This initiative follows the central government's decision to end the Smart City Mission in December 2025, necessitating the transfer of ongoing projects to the civic body.

news.title: Overhaul of Indiramma housing scheme: Telangana housing minister
news.para: Housing Minister Ponguleti Srinivas Reddy announced a significant overhaul of the Indiramma housing scheme, empowering district collectors to expedite sanctions. Special officers will be appointed for better coordination, and new applications will be accepted. MLAs are urged to ensure timely construction, with unutilized units to be reassigned.

news.title: Construction & land deals banned in parts of Gaya till March 2027
news.para: Construction and land transactions are banned in parts of Gaya district until March 31, 2027, to facilitate a satellite township and master plan. The restrictions impact 20 wards in Bodh Gaya Nagar Parishad and 80 revenue villages, aiming to curb haphazard development and guide urban expansion.

news.title: Over 490 plots up for grabs in Lucknow development body's Anantnagar scheme
news.para: The Lucknow Development Authority (LDA) is opening the second phase of online registration for 498 residential plots in its Anantnagar scheme from April 28 to May 27. Allotment will be via lottery, with applicants needing to pay a 5% registration fee. This 785-acre project on Mohan Road features a grid layout, wide roads, and underground power lines.

news.title: Gurugram: HSVP puts on hold demolition drive, says awaiting HC instructions
news.para: Gurugram's anti-encroachment drive is paused. Haryana Shahari Vikas Pradhikaran awaits High Court guidance. The court is hearing a petition on stilt-plus-four-floor construction. This policy is linked to many encroachment cases. The drive aims to clear public land of illegal structures. It will resume after clear court orders are received.

news.title: Nashik civic body starts process to cancel 26 tentative layouts linked to MHADA scam
news.para: Nashik Municipal Corporation is cancelling 26 tentative housing layouts. This action follows a probe into the MHADA housing scam. Police have booked 197 individuals. A special investigation team is also looking into the matter. Land records show these layouts are not on official base maps. This move aims to address violations of housing norms.

news.title: Johnson Lifts takes majority control of Toshiba JV, targets doubling of topline
news.para: Johnson Lifts has acquired a majority stake in its joint venture with Toshiba Elevator Corporation, increasing its holding to over 80%. This strategic move positions the company for expansion in India's premium elevator segment, leveraging Toshiba's technology for high-rise buildings. The joint venture aims to double its revenue and increase market share in the growing Indian elevator market.
